Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft Sara E. Madison To Answer Interrogatories , To Produce Documents, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-19-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Jacqueline Halson's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and request for production of documents is moot as responses have been served. Failure to respond in a timely fashion and to make a good faith and reasonable effort to informally resolve the matters in dispute was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $740 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Default Prove-Up In Scd.
Off Calendar Feb-15-2005 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Judge Ollie Marie-Victoire, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Anthony Vaughn.
James Green, Esq. for Plaintiff: Jacqueline D. Halson, present, was sworn and examined.
After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant: Sara E. Madison as follows:
Damages $175,000.00
Punitive Damages $175,000.00
Costs $655.35 for a total of $350,655.35
Judgment signed in open court.
